author: cs_student_22
message: Great intro to CS. Prof Henz is amazing. Workload is heavy but manageable. About 10 hours per week. The concepts are fundamental and well-taught. Got an A-.

author: struggling_eng
message: As an engineering student this was really tough. No prior programming experience and the pace was fast. Spent 18 hours a week and still only got a C+. The concepts are interesting but I felt left behind.

author: cs_student_22
message: Standard calculus module. Not too bad if you did well in JC math. About 5-6 hours per week. Exams are straightforward. Got a B+.

author: algo_lover
message: Loved this module. OOP and functional programming concepts are well-covered. Prof is clear and the labs are fun. About 10 hours per week. Got an A.

author: night_owl_coder
message: Decent module but the labs can be frustrating. Sometimes unclear what's expected. Teaching was okay, not great. About 8 hours per week. Got a B.

author: struggling_eng
message: Statistics is not my thing but this was manageable. The workload was light compared to CS modules. About 5 hours per week. Got a B-. Prof was decent.

author: pragmatic_senior
message: Software engineering module. Very practical and directly applicable to industry. Group project heavy. About 12 hours per week but spikes during project deadlines. Got an A-. Highly recommend.

author: burned_out
message: The group project was a nightmare. Workload was insane, easily 15-20 hours per week towards the end. I was so stressed. Content is useful but the execution of this module needs work. Got a B.

author: is_student
message: Good intro to IS. Covers a lot of ground. Not too technical. About 6 hours per week. The group project was interesting. Got an A-.

author: career_focused
message: Communication module paired with CS2103T. Light workload, about 4 hours per week. Presentations and writing. Easy A if you put in effort. Not intellectually stimulating but practical for career.

author: algo_lover
message: The natural follow-up to CS2040. Much more theoretical and proof-heavy. If you enjoyed 2040 you'll love this. Workload about 12 hours per week. Very challenging but intellectually satisfying. Got an A-.

author: overachiever_99
message: Computer organization. Straightforward if you attend lectures. About 6 hours per week. Content is dry but important. Got an A. Not much to complain about.
